summary: 🎯 ROOT CAUSE FOUND for user's HTTP 500 on Super Admin 'Approve' → RACE CONDITION between two concurrent PATCH /api/company-requests/{id} calls (double-tap / mobile network retry). All 7 requested edge cases (idempotent re-approve, live-firm phone collision → 409, super_admin phone → 409, missing contact_mobile → 400, legacy no-kind / null business_category → 200, duplicate company_name → 200, missing office_lat/lng → 200) PASS individually with correct status codes. Mobile-header parity and preview-URL parity also PASS. But 5/5 runs of the concurrent-approve test reliably reproduce the user's exact HTTP 500 with an uncaught pymongo.errors.DuplicateKeyError E11000 on the users.email_1 unique index at server.py:5726. The Iter-86 idempotency guard at lines 5566-5585 only protects SERIAL re-taps (both reads see status='pending' in a race, so both fall through to the provisioning block, one winner does insert_one, the loser crashes). Full test suite: 12/12 pass isolated (race test's pass depends on scheduler timing when suite-warmed up). Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter88_company_request_approval.py, JUnit: /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_iter88.xml.

critical.issue: RACE CONDITION → HTTP 500. Two concurrent Approve calls (mobile double-tap or network retry) BOTH read req.status='pending' before either has written the new status. Both fall through the Iter-86 idempotency guard (server.py:5566-5585) and enter the provisioning block. The winner inserts the company + admin user; the loser crashes with pymongo.errors.DuplicateKeyError E11000 on the users.email_1 unique index at server.py:5726 → uncaught → uvicorn returns HTTP 500 with empty body → mobile client's ApiClient falls back to 'HTTP 500' error message. Also latent risk of DuplicateKeyError on companies.company_code_1 (server.py:5706, generated from 6-char hex uuid — statistically rare but possible on high traffic).

ui_bugs.issue: The `busy` state is set AFTER onPress fires, but is not guarded per-request-id at the button level. Rapid double-tap or a slow network + user impatience can dispatch two PATCH calls before setBusy takes effect / before the first response returns, exposing the backend race. Also does optimistic status flip which may confuse users into re-tapping.

integration_issues.issue: Frontend + backend jointly susceptible to double-fire → 500. See RCA above.

action_items: [BACKEND-CRITICAL] Fix the race in decide_company_request (server.py:5541-5750). Recommended pattern — atomic Compare-And-Set BEFORE provisioning: `res = await db.company_requests.update_one({'request_id': request_id, 'status': 'pending'}, {'$set': {'status': 'provisioning', 'decided_by': user['user_id'], 'decided_at': now_iso()}})`. If `res.matched_count == 0` → someone else is (or has already) provisioned → re-read the doc and return the idempotent already_decided payload. Only when matched_count == 1 proceed with company + user creation, then final `update_one` to flip status to 'approved'. This eliminates the race entirely.

[BACKEND-DEFENSIVE] Additionally wrap the `await db.users.insert_one(user_doc)` and `await db.companies.insert_one(company_doc)` calls (server.py:5706, 5726) in `try/except pymongo.errors.DuplicateKeyError` and retry once with a fresh company_code (for the companies unique index) or short-circuit with the existing admin user (for the users email/phone unique indexes). Belt-and-braces for any residual race and for the accidental company_code hex collision.

[BACKEND-OBSERVABILITY] Add `logger.exception(...)` around the provisioning block so that when the next 500 happens the traceback is emitted with the request_id — currently the trace only shows the framework stack and the specific DuplicateKeyError, not the request_id being processed.

[FRONTEND] In app/company-requests.tsx (~L70), set `setBusy(id)` SYNCHRONOUSLY before the async fetch, and disable the button when `busy === id` OR when `r.status !== 'pending'` (defensive against optimistic-update race). Also debounce onPress by 300ms.
[FRONTEND] Consider showing the server error `detail` inline in the modal instead of the generic `HTTP 500` fallback — helps users report actionable bugs.

[USER-COMMUNICATION] The Iter-86 fix's guard only handles SERIAL re-taps. If the user is on 3G/flaky wifi, or if they double-tapped Approve, they will still hit the race. Advise them to tap once and wait; once the backend fix ships this will no longer matter.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:5541-5750 decide_company_request — read-modify-write pattern with no atomicity. Doc read at L5553, provisioning between L5688-5730, doc update at L5732-5745. Any concurrent execution can duplicate the provisioning block. Must be converted to CAS.

server.py:5566-5585 — 'Iter 86 - Idempotency guard' correctly handles the SEQUENTIAL re-approve case (req.status already == 'approved' OR req.result_company_id set) but is bypassed when both callers are in-flight before the first write commits.

server.py:5687-5705 — company_code = uuid.uuid4().hex[:6].upper() has ~1/16M collision probability; unique index on company_code will 500 on collision. Should retry with a fresh code on DuplicateKeyError.

server.py:5711 phone stored as `req['contact_mobile']` (already normalised), but multiple `_normalise_phone` variants get created at approval time (L5607-5614). Not a bug per se; just noting for completeness.

app/company-requests.tsx L70-110 — `busy` is a single string state, but the button only checks `busy===r.request_id`. React setState is async → two fast taps in the same JS tick both see busy=null and both fire. Move the guard into a ref or use setState functional form.

src/api/client.ts L109-113 — `HTTP ${res.status}` fallback is fine, but when body is present but not JSON-parseable the fallback swallows the raw text. Consider surfacing the first ~200 chars of `text` when data isn't a dict.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter88_company_request_approval.py (NEW — 12 tests covering all 7 edge cases + race + mobile header parity + preview URL parity)

success_rate.backend: 11/12 endpoint edge cases PASS. 1 test (TestConcurrentApproveRace) reliably fails (5/5 isolated runs → HTTP 500) — this IS the user's reproducer.

seed_data_creation: All test-created firms/users/requests are prefixed `TEST_` and are torn down in each test's cleanup block. Post-run DB has 1 legacy 'APP TEST Firm' company + 0 pending requests. Cleaned up 16 stale TEST_* companies, 5 stale test users, 5 stale test requests from earlier failed pytest runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Race test lives at TestConcurrentApproveRace in /app/backend/tests/test_iter88_company_request_approval.py — it fires 3 concurrent PATCH approves via ThreadPoolExecutor and asserts no 5xx. After the main agent applies the CAS fix, RE-RUN JUST this test 10× in a row to confirm no flakes: `for i in {1..10}; do pytest backend/tests/test_iter88_company_request_approval.py::TestConcurrentApproveRace -q; done`. All 10 must pass. Also re-run the full suite: `pytest backend/tests/test_iter88_company_request_approval.py -v`. Preview URL for mobile parity: https://emplo-connect-1.preview.emergentagent.com/api. Super admin OTP token generation: POST /api/auth/otp/request with `{identifier: 'sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com', channel: 'email'}` → dev_code in body → POST /api/auth/otp/verify → session_token.
rca_of_issue:
REPRODUCTION STEPS (from super_admin OTP session):
1. POST /api/auth/company-register (kind=self_register, pin='294817', unique phone+email) → 200, returns req_id.
2. Fire 3× PATCH /api/company-requests/{req_id} {status:'approved'} concurrently via ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=3).
3. Observed: [200, 500, 500]. The 500 responses have empty JSON body → mobile client renders 'HTTP 500'.

EXACT TRACEBACK (from /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log):
  File '/app/backend/server.py', line 5726, in decide_company_request
    await db.users.insert_one(user_doc)
  ...
  pymongo.errors.DuplicateKeyError: E11000 duplicate key error collection: test_database.users index: email_1 dup key: { email: 'race_b69279@test.local' }

WHY IT HAPPENS (line-by-line):
  - L5553: both requests read the SAME `req` doc with status='pending', result_company_id=None.
  - L5566-5585: idempotency guard checks `req.get('status') == 'approved'` AND `req.get('result_company_id')` — both are falsy for a fresh pending request read by BOTH callers before either writes → guard passes both through.
  - L5615-5618: both find _existing=[] because no user with that phone yet.
  - L5686-5726: both proceed to build company_doc + user_doc.
  - Winner: L5706 (insert company) + L5726 (insert user) succeed → L5732 update_one flips status='approved'.
  - Loser (T+few ms later): L5706 either succeeds (unique on company_code by chance) OR fails (rare). Then L5726 tries to insert user_doc with the SAME `contact_email` (unique index on users.email_1) → DuplicateKeyError → uncaught → HTTP 500 with empty body.

MITIGATION (main agent to implement):
  A) Atomic CAS at entry: `res = await db.company_requests.update_one({'request_id': request_id, 'status': 'pending'}, {'$set': {'status': 'provisioning'}})`. If matched_count==0, someone else beat us → re-read doc and return already_decided idempotent 200. Only when matched_count==1 do we proceed to insert company + user. Final update at L5732 flips 'provisioning' → 'approved'.
  B) Defensive try/except DuplicateKeyError around insert_one calls (5706 & 5726) to convert to 409 with a useful message and to auto-retry company_code on hex collision.

MOBILE APP TRIGGER (why user still sees this despite Iter-86 fix):
  - The Iter-86 fix guards serial double-clicks (works well: idempotent 200 with already_decided=true — verified by TestIdempotentReapprove).
  - But mobile users on flaky 3G/wifi tap Approve, wait ~1s with no response, tap AGAIN → RN dispatches BOTH fetches concurrently → race → 500 on the 2nd.
  - Also possible: RN's onPress firing twice within a single tap if the button doesn't set `busy` synchronously (setState is async).

FRONTEND FALLBACK: /app/frontend/src/api/client.ts:109-113 falls back to `HTTP ${res.status}` when the response has no JSON body — exactly what user sees in their screenshot ('Company request' / 'HTTP 500').

NOTE: The main agent's earlier claim that 'curl end-to-end works' is TRUE for serial calls. The bug only manifests under concurrency. Their curl test did not exercise the race.
